title: Floresville
name: Floresville
metaTitle: Floresville 1031 Exchange Services in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Floresville investors evaluating Eagle Ford energy-support land, agricultural acreage, and courthouse-square commercial.

sections.html: Floresville is the Wilson County seat southeast of San Antonio, where a historic courthouse square, agricultural land along the San Antonio River, and Eagle Ford Shale energy-support activity along US-181 and FM-536 combine into a commercial base unlike anywhere closer to the metro core. An investor considering Floresville should understand these three demand drivers separately, since they behave on different cycles.

sections.heading: Eagle Ford Energy Support and Its Cyclical Risk
sections.html:
Laydown yards, equipment storage, and service commercial along US-181 grew directly out of Eagle Ford Shale drilling activity, and that demand moves with energy prices rather than local population growth. An investor evaluating an energy-support parcel should request occupancy and rent history covering at least one prior downturn, since a lease signed during peak drilling activity may not reflect the property's baseline performance.

Some of these parcels were built to a specific operator's specifications, including reinforced pads or fencing that add cost to convert for a different tenant, so a property-condition assessment should evaluate re-tenanting cost as part of the underwriting rather than treating the building as generic industrial space.

sections.heading: Agricultural Acreage Along the San Antonio River
sections.html:
Ranch and agricultural acreage remains a significant share of Wilson County's land base, and an investor identifying agricultural land as replacement property should confirm current use and any agricultural tax exemption status, since a change in use can trigger rollback taxes that need to be accounted for separately from the exchange itself. Water rights and floodplain determinations along the river corridor are also worth confirming before identification.

Fencing, cross-lease agreements with a grazing tenant, and any easement granted to a utility or pipeline company are common on ranch acreage in this county, and each should be documented in the title search, since an unrecorded grazing arrangement can complicate an investor's plans to change the land's use after closing.

sections.heading: What Trades Around the Courthouse Square
sections.html:
A realistic Floresville identification list draws from a mix that includes:

  • energy-support commercial and laydown yards along US-181
  • agricultural and ranch acreage near the river corridor
  • small retail and office around the courthouse square
  • service commercial along FM-536

sections.heading: Title and Mineral Rights Documentation
sections.html:
Because of the county's oil and gas history, a title search on Floresville property should confirm whether mineral rights were severed from the surface estate at some point, since a severed mineral interest can affect both financing and future surface use even when current drilling activity is limited. A Phase I environmental review is also standard here given the presence of prior energy-related uses on some parcels.

A surface use agreement, if one exists, should also be reviewed in full rather than summarized secondhand, since these agreements often grant an operator ongoing access rights that a new owner cannot simply terminate, and a lender will want that document reviewed before extending financing on the purchase.

sections.heading: Timing a Purchase Against Energy and Agricultural Cycles
sections.html: Given how tied Floresville's commercial performance is to Eagle Ford activity, we recommend reviewing current rig counts and operator activity in the county before identifying an energy-support property, rather than underwriting off a lease signed during a prior peak. Investors often pair a Floresville energy-support candidate with an agricultural or courthouse-square backup under the three-property rule, since these asset types respond to different demand cycles and rarely move together.

faqs.q: Does Eagle Ford energy activity make Floresville commercial property riskier than typical industrial space?
faqs.a: It introduces cycle risk tied to energy prices rather than local population growth, so occupancy and rent history should be reviewed across at least one prior downturn rather than relying solely on peak-period lease terms.

faqs.q: Can agricultural land in Floresville be used as 1031 replacement property?
faqs.a: Yes, ranch and agricultural acreage held for investment qualifies as like-kind to other real property, but current agricultural tax exemption status should be confirmed, since a change in use can trigger rollback taxes separate from the exchange itself.

faqs.q: Why does mineral rights history matter for a Floresville title search?
faqs.a: Wilson County's oil and gas history means mineral rights were sometimes severed from the surface estate. That severance can affect financing and future surface use, so it should be confirmed before a property is identified, not after closing.

faqs.q: Should I pair an energy-support property with a different asset type on my identification list?
faqs.a: Often, yes. Energy-support and agricultural or courthouse-square commercial respond to different demand cycles, so combining them under the three-property rule reduces the risk of a single cycle affecting the entire exchange.

faqs.q: Who manages my exchange funds during a Floresville closing with mineral rights questions to resolve?
faqs.a: A qualified intermediary holds the funds throughout the 180-day exchange period regardless of how long title questions take to resolve, as long as closing occurs within that period. The investor never takes direct receipt of the funds.

faqs.q: What if a Floresville ranch parcel has an existing surface use agreement with an energy operator?
faqs.a: That agreement should be reviewed in full before identification, since it can grant an operator ongoing access rights that a new owner cannot simply terminate. Your lender will want the document reviewed before financing is extended.

faqs.q: How should I evaluate a courthouse-square property compared to an energy-support parcel in Floresville?
faqs.a: The two behave on different cycles: courthouse-square commercial tracks local population and county government activity, while energy-support parcels track Eagle Ford drilling activity. Comparing them directly on cap rate alone misses this underlying difference in risk.
